query: has ORMD-0801 been tested in humans

retrieved_docs.title: 21-Hydroxylase Deficiency -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Atumelnant, an oral melanocortin type 2 receptor (ACTH receptor) antagonist, is being studied in a dose-finding open-label phase 2 study. [26] Initial findings suggested a profound and sustained decrease in the participants' morning androstenedione and 17-hydroxyprogesterone. Lu AG13909, an ACTH monoclonal antibody available in IV formulation, is being studied in a phase 1, open-label study to assess its effects on morning 17-hydroxyprogesterone, androstenedione, and ACTH levels ( NIHR. UCLH patient first in world to receive a new investigational drug in a phase 1 trial ). BBP-631, a gene-based therapy currently in early-stage development, aims to treat the condition most physiologically by promoting endogenous cortisol production by the adrenals  ( bridgebio. BridgeBio Pharma Reports Topline Results from Phase 1/2 Trial of Investigational Gene Therapy for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) ).

retrieved_docs.title: Andexanet Alfa -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Breastfeeding considerations: No clinical data are available on the use of andexanet alfa during breastfeeding. As a large protein molecule (~40,000 Da), its concentration in milk is expected to be minimal, and absorption is unlikely due to potential degradation in the infant’s gastrointestinal tract. Until further data are available, caution is advised when administering andexanet alfa during breastfeeding, particularly in preterm or neonatal infants. [16] Pediatric patients: The safety and efficacy of andexanet alfa have not been investigated in the pediatric population. Older patients: No significant differences in safety or efficacy have been observed in older patients, although increased sensitivity in the geriatric population cannot be ruled out.

retrieved_docs.title: Andexanet Alfa -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy considerations: Andexanet alfa has not been studied in pregnant women, and reproductive or developmental studies in animals are unavailable; thus, potential fetal risks remain unknown. Safety and efficacy of andexanet alfa during labor and delivery have not been established.

retrieved_docs.title: Anifrolumab  -- Administration -- Specific Patient Population
retrieved_docs.content: Breastfeeding women: No information is available on the clinical use of anifrolumab during breastfeeding. As anifrolumab is a large protein molecule, the amount in milk is likely to be very low. Anifroluman may be destroyed in the infant's gastrointestinal tract and is probably minimally absorbed. [21] Pediatric patients: No dose adjustment information is available for pediatric patients as no safety and efficacy were evaluated for patients younger than 18.

retrieved_docs.title: Keratoconjunctivitis -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials -- Key Pertinent Studies
retrieved_docs.content: Lifitegrast Clinical Trials (OPUS-1, OPUS-2, OPUS-3): These clinical trials investigated the efficacy and safety of lifitegrast, an antagonist of lymphocyte function-associated antigen 1 (LFA-1), in reducing DED signs and symptoms. Significant improvements in ocular surface staining and patient-reported symptoms were observed, supporting its approval for the treatment of moderate-to-severe dry eye. [96]

retrieved_docs.title: Linaclotide -- Mechanism of Action -- Pharmacokinetics
retrieved_docs.content: Food effect: In a clinical trial, neither linaclotide nor its active metabolite MM-419447 was detected in plasma after oral intake of 290 mcg once-a-day dosage for 7 days in both fed and non-fed states in healthy volunteers.

retrieved_docs.title: Andexanet Alfa -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the indications for the use of andexanet alfa. Assess the dosing parameters for andexanet alfa. Evaluate the mechanism of action of andexanet alfa. Implement effective collaboration and communication among interprofessional team members to improve outcomes and treatment efficacy for patients who might benefit from andexanet alfa therapy. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Romosozumab -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Breastfeeding considerations: Romosozumab is not approved for women of reproductive potential. In animal studies with pregnant rats, romosozumab administration beginning 6 weeks before mating and lactation resulted in detectable serum levels in offspring, confirming dose-dependent exposure during gestation and lactation. Pediatric patients: The safety and efficacy of romosozumab have not been established in pediatric patients.

retrieved_docs.title: Aducanumab -- Monitoring
retrieved_docs.content: To date, no adequate study of aducanumab use during pregnancy and lactation in humans has been performed. Aducanumab was tested during animal reproductive studies, and no adverse effects were detected, although β-amyloid is not present in rat. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Inotersen -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Breastfeeding considerations: There is no data regarding the presence of inotersen in human milk, milk production, or the effects on the breastfed infant. However, a study in lactating mice has shown that inotersen is excreted in milk. Perform a risk-benefit analysis for lactating women and infants treated with inotersen. Pediatric patients: The safety and effectiveness of inotersen are not established in pediatric patients.

retrieved_docs.title: Anifrolumab  -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Anifrolumab was 1 of 3 anti-type-1 interferon agents under investigation as a potential treatment for systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). Initial in vivo studies observed higher levels of serum interferon (IFN) in patients with autoimmune disease as opposed to those of healthy controls. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Anifrolumab  -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Anifrolumab  -- Mechanism of Action -- Pharmacokinetics
retrieved_docs.content: Elimination: The clearance of anifrolumab is medicated by IFNAR1 and exhibits non-linear pharmacokinetics at higher doses. The estimated systemic clearance (CL) for anifrolumab was 0.193 L/d based on a healthy volunteer study at 300 mg intravenous dose every 4 weeks.

retrieved_docs.title: Setmelanotide -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Setmelanotide is not recommended for use while breastfeeding as there is no data available on the presence of setmelanotide or its metabolites in human milk, the effects on the breastfed infant, or the effects on milk production. [23] However, a nonclinical study showed that setmelanotide is excreted in the milk of nursing rats; hence, it is likely to be present in human milk; therefore, the potential risk to the newborn cannot be excluded. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Lemborexant -- Administration -- Normal Dosing Recommendations
retrieved_docs.content: Lemborexant is administered orally in doses of either 5 mg or 10 mg immediately before bed. Patients should not use lemborexant if they anticipate waking up before 7 hours after taking the medication so as not to experience dangerous impairment while awake. The maximum recommended clinical dose of lemborexant should not exceed 10 mg. Additionally, taking this medication with food or before eating may delay its onset of action. Lemborexant is not meant for use in combination with alcoholic beverages. Furthermore, there is no available research on lemborexant in pediatric populations or pregnant or breastfeeding women.

retrieved_docs.title: Drug Trials -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: Phase 0 trials are optional, exploratory, first-in-human trials that are designed to determine whether the drug affects human subjects as expected from earlier pre-clinical investigations. These trials involve the administration of low, nontherapeutic doses of the study drug to a small number of participants (typically 10 to 15) and help gather preliminary data on the pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of the drug in human subjects. [4] [5]

Imidazoline‑receptor agonists, exemplified by moxonidine and rilmenidine, are also classified as centrally acting anti‑adrenergic drugs and are defined as inverse‑ISA of “product containing imidazoline receptor agonist,” underscoring their mechanism of action on imidazoline receptors. Methyldopa, another centrally acting anti‑adrenergic antihypertensive, shares this mechanism but is likewise contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity. An pharmacological overlap exists between rescinnamine and angiotensin‑converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors, suggesting that rescinnamine may influence ACE‑modulated pathways. Conversely, aliskiren’s renin‑inhibiting activity is contraindicated when combined with ACE‑inhibitor activity, highlighting the need to avoid concurrent use. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2762
retrieved_docs.content: PD 134308 is classified as a hormone antagonist, indicating that it functions by blocking hormone receptors. Atosiban is identified as a substance with a peptide hormone receptor antagonist mechanism of action and is also categorized as a hormone antagonist. These relationships show that both PD 134308 and atosiban share the functional property of antagonizing hormone receptors, a characteristic that is important for therapeutic approaches aimed at modulating hormone‑mediated physiological processes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 984
retrieved_docs.content: Methyldopa, centrally acting adntiadrenergic agents is classified as an antiadrenergic agents, centrally acting (using an “isa” relationship) and is a member of the broader methyldopa category. Both methyldopa and the non‑steroidal anti‑inflammatory drug fenoprofen have a contraindication for patients with فرط تحسس (hypersensitivity), indicating that these agents should not be used in individuals who are hypersensitive. Similarly, the antifungal medication butenafine is contraindicated with the disease فرط تحسس, reinforcing the need to assess hypersensitivity status before prescribing these drugs. Recognizing and avoiding hypersensitivity reactions to methyldopa, fenoprofen, and butenafine is essential for safe patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 2 -- Community 425
retrieved_docs.content: the experimental compound PD‑134308 functions simultaneously as a hormonal antagonist and an antitumor agent directed against the target isa, exemplifying dual pharmacologic activity. This integrated framework delineates classifications, qualifying relationships, therapeutic scopes and specific contraindications of key antineoplastic and inhibitory drugs, supporting informed oncologic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 4 -- Community 2782
retrieved_docs.content: Amlexanox, benralizumab, and tezepelumab are all classified as members of the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) group “other systemic drugs for obstructive airway diseases.” This category encompasses systemic pharmacologic agents used to treat conditions characterized by airway obstruction, such as asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By belonging to the same ATC class, these three drugs share a therapeutic focus on modulating inflammatory pathways or other mechanisms to improve airway function, highlighting their related roles in the management of obstructive airway disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 2 -- Community 764
retrieved_docs.content: Canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab are both classified as epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors. EGFR inhibitors are a subset of protein kinase inhibitors that possess antineoplastic and immunomodulating properties, placing them within the broader therapeutic category of agents used to treat cancer and modulate immune responses.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1870
retrieved_docs.content: Icatibant is classified among the drugs used to treat hereditary angioedema, a rare genetic disorder characterized by episodic swelling of the skin and mucosal tissues. As a bradykinin B2‑receptor antagonist, icatibant specifically blocks the action of bradykinin, a key mediator of the swelling attacks in hereditary angioedema, thereby providing rapid relief of symptoms during acute episodes. Inclusion of icatibant in the therapeutic armamentarium underscores its role as a targeted pharmacologic option for managing this condition and improving patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2040
retrieved_docs.content: identified as an opioid analgesic, highlighting the therapeutic potential of kappa‑receptor targeting agents, which may offer analgesia with a different side‑effect profile compared with traditional mu‑agonists. Collectively, these entities demonstrate that opioid analgesics encompass a broad spectrum of dosage forms (injectable, oral solution, capsule), combination products, and both classic and investigational opioid molecules, all aimed at providing effective pain control across various clinical contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 3 -- Community 1660
retrieved_docs.content: Inhibitory fosfodiesteras are pharmacologic agents that act by inhibiting phosphodiesterase enzymes, thereby influencing intracellular cyclic nucleotide levels and related physiological processes. Both papaverin and theofylin are examples of inhibitory fosfodiesteras, sharing this mechanism of action. By reducing phosphodiesterase activity, these compounds can affect smooth muscle relaxation, platelet function, and other cellular responses, making their classification as inhibitory fosfodiesteras clinically relevant for understanding their therapeutic effects and potential applications in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2461
retrieved_docs.content: Drugs used in erectile dysfunction constitute a therapeutic class that includes sildenafil and udenafil, both of which are members of this category of medications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 483
retrieved_docs.content: Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors constitute a subclass of protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents, and this class includes the drugs ibrutinib and zanubrutinib. Ibrutinib, as a member of the BTK inhibitor group, may be used to treat سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن (chronic lymphocytic leukemia). Similarly, Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors are also classified within the broader category of protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents; this EGFR inhibitor group comprises agents such as canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab. Together, BTK inhibitors and EGFR inhibitors illustrate how distinct targeted therapies share common mechanistic foundations as protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulatory effects, underscoring their relevance in the treatment of various cancers and immune‑related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 1508
retrieved_docs.content: Other antifungals for topical use in ATC is a pharmacological class that comprises the agents butenafine and tolnaftát. Both butenafine and tolnaftát are topical antifungal medications, and each is contraindicated in patients with the hypersensitivity condition described as فرط تحسس. Recognizing that these two drugs share the same contraindication is essential for safe prescribing and effective patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2457
retrieved_docs.content: PD 134308 is classified as a hormone antagonist. Atosiban is likewise classified as a hormone antagonist and is a synthetic analogue that is derived from the natural peptide vasotocin. Both PD 134308 and atosiban belong to the hormone‑antagonist class, indicating that they act by blocking specific hormonal pathways, and the link between atosiban and vasotocin highlights the latter’s origin as a modified form of the endogenous hormone. This shared classification informs their pharmacological use as agents that inhibit hormone‑mediated effects.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 930
retrieved_docs.content: PD 134308 is classified as a hormone antagonist (antagonisté hormonů). Atosiban is also a member of the hormone antagonist class, as indicated by the inverse “isa” relationship. Estrogen antagonists (antagonisté estrogenu) are a subcategory of hormone antagonists, establishing a hierarchical link between estrogen‑specific antagonism and broader hormone antagonism. Centchroman is identified as an estrogen antagonist, placing it within both the estrogen antagonist subcategory and the larger grouping of “hormone antagonists and related agents.” This structure shows that PD 134308 and atosiban act as general hormone antagonists, while centchroman functions as a more specific estrogen antagonist, all of which are organized under the overarching category of hormone antagonists and related agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1312
retrieved_docs.content: Atosiban is classified as a substance that exerts its effects through a peptide hormone receptor antagonist mechanism of action, placing it within the broader category of hormone antagonists. This classification highlights its role in blocking specific peptide hormone receptors, which is central to its therapeutic use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1863
retrieved_docs.content: N [Preparations] is a class of pharmaceutical preparations that includes the agents nedokromil and nusinersen. Both mupirocin and nusinersen produce the physiologic effect of increased protein synthesis, indicating that nusinersen, as a member of N [Preparations], shares this effect with mupirocin. Recognizing the categorical relationship of nedokromil and nusinersen within N [Preparations] and the shared protein‑synthesis‑enhancing action of nusinersen and mupirocin can aid in understanding their mechanisms and guiding therapeutic decisions.
